detaileddescription.para: This set of templates defines converters. The conversions are from string to object, object to string, and binary buffer to object, object to binary buffer. Many classes convert through a single template. Strings and other things with additional data need special converters. The key point is that the serialized form (buffer or output string) must have a complete independent copy of the data. So if a pointer or reference type is converted, then there must be a complete copy made.
Conv is used only during while the memory for the argument is stable. You can't create a Conv object from a variable, and free the variable before using the Conv.
